texto: As you get older you strip away the things you don't have time for, and then you are left with only the things you have time for. Your life gets skinnier and skinnier until you wonder why you go on. You go on because there are things that must get done. You become no longer a person so much as a place, an unfunny place where things come to get done.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Jonathan Goldstein sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “As you get older you strip away the things you don't have time for, and then you are left with on…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a humor, aging, vida, útil em momentos de humor e vida.

texto: When David wasn't ruling, he would ponder all the various forms of laughter there could be. So far, he had only categorized four: laughter at your own expense, laughter at the expense of others, laughter at the human predicament, and laughter at small animals falling off tables.
explicacao: Citação de Jonathan Goldstein sobre temas ligados a humor. “When David wasn't ruling, he would ponder all the various forms of laughter there could be. So fa…” articula humor, laughter, expense em registro de humor, com relevância para contextos como laughter.

texto: By going "ah" and "hah" they were able to lift the unrelenting pain of their dark, bestial days into something more recreational. It is only through the godly gift of humor that man endures the horror. What other faculty allows you to turn pain into triumph? Tears of sadness into tears of laughing too hard?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Jonathan Goldstein que provoca temas ligados a humor. Ao questionar “By going "ah" and "hah" they were able to lift the unrelenting pain of their dark, bestial days i…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, laughter, tears, com foco em termino e reflexao-noturna.

texto: Everyone runs around trying to find a place where they still serve breakfast because eating breakfast, even if it's 5 o'clock in the afternoon, is a sign that the day has just begun and good things can still happen. Having lunch is like throwing in the towel.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jonathan Goldstein: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Everyone runs around trying to find a place where they still serve breakfast because eating break…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, breakfast, esperanca pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de esperanca e lunch.

texto: When he heard his father call out for Abel and he saw his borther go forth, it made him feel like he was nothing. He couldn’t even say that he felt like Cain anymore. One could not feel like Cain because it had no flavor. Cain was the absence of flavor. Cain was like saliva or a Wednesday.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jonathan Goldstein: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “When he heard his father call out for Abel and he saw his borther go forth, it made him feel like…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, bible, old-testament pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de old-testament e sibling-rivalry.

texto: The first hands he heard banging at the outside walls felt like nails pushing into his temples. Then there were more hands. Pounding. Punching. Scratching. Then kicks and shrieking that even drowned out the sound of the rain.The worst was when Ham could make out individual voices. He could hear their neighbor Zebeleh and her little daughter Ariel
